question.content: For the two-stage direct-coupled BJT amplifier with beta1=beta2=100 and rbb=300 ohm, compute key Q-point values and overall small-signal metrics. Output exactly eight comma-separated numbers in the order IBQ1_uA,ICQ1_mA,UCEQ1_V,ICQ2_mA,UCEQ2_V,Av,Ri_kOhm,Ro_kOhm; keep IBQ1_uA to one digit after the decimal point, ICQ1_mA/UCEQ1_V/ICQ2_mA/UCEQ2_V/Ri_kOhm/Ro_kOhm to two digits after the decimal point, and Av as an integer; plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no percent sign, no fraction, no equation form, and no extra text.

step_1.content: Perform sequential DC bias analysis for stage 1 and stage 2 due to direct coupling.
step_1.key_point: Perform sequential DC bias analysis for stage 1 and stage 2 due to direct coupling.

step_3.content: Analyze panel 1 of 2 and record the intermediate relation needed before moving to the next panel.
step_3.key_point: Set up the governing relation for key Q-point values and overall small-signal metrics.

step_5.content: Use the small-signal model to compute Av, Ri, Ro and report all values.
step_5.key_point: The small-signal model to compute Av, Ri, Ro.
